models.name: person_sumtotal
models.description: This table is the master record for every individual in the system. It serves as the central source of truth for a person's identity, where the term 'person' is used synonymously with 'user', 'learner', and 'employee'. All other tables that track user activity, such as attempts or job details or Activity Assignments etc, link back to this table to identify the specific individual involved. Use this table when you need to: 1. Identify and authenticate users in the system, 2. Generate user profiles and demographic reports, 3. Track individual learning paths and personalization, 4. Manage user accounts and access permissions, 5. Create personalized learning experiences, 6. Generate user-specific compliance and progress reports, 7. Handle user onboarding and offboarding processes.

properties.description: Unique identifier for each person in the person_sumtotal table.
A unique identifier for individuals in the person_sumtotal table.
Column representing the last name of a person.
The first name of the person, represented as a text string.
The middle name of a person, stored as a variable character string.
The full name of the person, combining first, middle, and last names.
Indicates whether a person is currently active in the system.
Column representing the gender of a person, stored as a text value.
The date of birth of the person, stored as a string in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
A code representing the company associated with a person.
The name of the company associated with the person.
The date when a person record becomes effective, represented as a string in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
The date when a person's record is considered inactive or no longer valid.
Indicates whether a person record has been deleted.
Date when the record was created in the system.

This table is the master record for every individual in the system. It serves as the central source of truth for a person's identity, where the term 'person' is used synonymously with 'user', 'learner', and 'employee'. All other tables that track user activity, such as attempts or job details or Activity Assignments etc, link back to this table to identify the specific individual involved.
Links persons to organizations through the personorganization junction table
Links persons to their learning attempts
